heading: “President” as including agencies, officers and employees
label: 715j “President” as including agencies, officers and employees
text: Wherever reference is made in this chapter to the President such reference shall be held to include, in addition to the President, any agency, officer, or employee who may be designated by the President for the execution of any of the powers and functions vested in the President under this chapter.
notes: Delegation of FunctionsEx. Ord. No. 6979, , which designated and appointed Secretary of the Interior to execute powers and functions vested in President by this chapter except those vested in him by , was superseded by Ex. Ord. No. 10752, set out below.Feb. 28, 1935section 715c of this titleEx. Ord. No. 7756, , 2 F.R. 2664, which delegated to Secretary of the Interior powers and functions vested in President under this chapter except those vested in him by , and authorized Secretary to establish a Petroleum Conservation Division in Department of the Interior, the functions and duties of which shall be: (1) to assist, in such manner as may be prescribed by Secretary of the Interior, in administering said act, (2) to cooperate with oil and gas-producing States in prevention of waste in oil and gas production and in adoption of uniform oil- and gas-conservation laws and regulations, and (3) to keep informed currently as to facts which may be required for exercise of responsibility of President under , was superseded by Ex. Ord. No. 10752, set out below.Dec. 1, 1937section 715c of this titlesection 715c of this title

Ex. Ord. No. 10752. Delegation of Functions to the Secretary of the InteriorEx. Ord. No. 10752, , 23 F.R. 973, provided:Feb. 12, 19581. The Secretary of the Interior is hereby designated and appointed as the agent of the President for the execution of all the powers and functions vested in the President by the act of , , entitled “An Act to regulate interstate and foreign commerce in petroleum and its products by prohibiting the shipment in such commerce of petroleum and its products produced in violation of State law, and for other purposes,” as amended ( .), except those vested in the President by section 4 of the act ().SectionFebruary 22, 193549 Stat. 3015 U.S.C. 71515 U.S.C. 715cet seq. 2. The Secretary of the Interior may make such provisions in the Department of the Interior as he may deem appropriate to administer the said act.Sec. 3. This Executive order supersedes Executive Order No. 6979 of , Executive Order No. 7756 of (2 F.R. 2664), Executive Order No. 9732 of (11 F.R. 5985), and paragraph (q) of section 1 of Executive Order No. 10250 of (16 F.R. 5385).SecFebruary 28, 1935December 1, 1937June 3, 1946June 5, 1951Dwight D. Eisenhower.
